city_spotlight.label: City Spotlight (map_info text)
city_spotlight.description: AI-written city profile for the map_info block's info panel, grounded in researched city facts (market_blurb, employers, industries, population, neighborhoods). Enrich type — fills mi_info_text in place; attach to a map_info block by setting its ai_type_id to city_spotlight.

city_intro.label: City Intro
city_intro.description: Standalone SEO intro (H2 + 2 paragraphs, 160-190 words) about the service in the target city.
city_intro.ai_prompt:
You are writing SEO content for {business}, a trained, locally operating water and storm damage restoration company serving {city}, {state}. Target keyword: "{service} {city}". Write a city intro section: 1. heading_text (H2): 6-10 words, includes "{city}" and references {service} or local demand for it. 2. text: exactly 2 flowing paragraphs, 160-190 words total. - Para 1: Why property owner in {city} need {service}, grounded in the region's weather, storm and flooding patterns, and seasonal freeze and thaw risks. - Para 2: How {business} helps with {service} specifically — approach, thoroughness, and follow-up. Keep the focus on {service}; you may briefly note they also handle other pest problems, but do NOT list them. End forward-looking. Use "{city}" 2-3 times. No bullets, no headings inside text. Wrap each paragraph in

...

. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not guarantee specific drying times or that all water and storm damage can be fully reversed. Do not promise insurance claim approval or coverage. Do not claim specific licenses, certifications, or accreditations. Do not make mold health-hazard claims or offer mold remediation, which is handled separately. Do not give unsafe do-it-yourself advice for standing water, sewage, or electrical hazards. Tone: reassuring and professional, with clear urgency for active water and storm emergencies. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"heading_text": "...", "text": "

...

...

"}

hero_subtext.label: Hero Subtext (Inject)
hero_subtext.description: Injects 2-3 city-specific sentences into the next hero_split block's hs_subtext field.
hero_subtext.ai_prompt:
You are writing hero subtext for {business}, a trained, locally operating water and storm damage restoration company, for a page targeting {city}, {state}. Write 2-3 sentences (40-60 words total): - Open with a {city}-specific statement grounded in the region's weather, storm and flooding patterns, and seasonal freeze and thaw risks. - Center on {service} — the specific problem {business} solves on this page. Do NOT list other pest types. - Close with a value statement for property owner. No bullets. No exclamation marks. Professional, not sales-y. Do not promise insurance claim approval or coverage. Do not claim specific licenses, certifications, or accreditations. Do not make mold health-hazard claims or offer mold remediation, which is handled separately. Do not give unsafe do-it-yourself advice for standing water, sewage, or electrical hazards. Tone: reassuring and professional, with clear urgency for active water and storm emergencies. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"text": "2-3 sentences here"}

feature_columns_local.label: Feature Columns - Local (Standalone)
feature_columns_local.description: 4-column feature_columns block: 3 core benefits + 1 city-specific column.
feature_columns_local.ai_prompt:

Write exactly 4 columns: - Columns 1-3: core benefits of {business}'s {service} - things like experience, responsiveness, guarantees, thorough treatment, and trained technicians. Do NOT list other pest types. - Column 4: a {city}-specific benefit grounded in the region's weather, storm and flooding patterns, and seasonal freeze and thaw risks. Each column: heading 3-5 words (benefit-focused); text 20-30 words, concrete. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

faq_local.label: FAQ - Local (Inject)
faq_local.description: Appends 2 city-specific FAQ items to the next faq_two_col block's fq_items array.
faq_local.ai_prompt:
You are writing FAQ content for {business}, a trained, locally operating water and storm damage restoration company, for a {city}, {state} page about {service}. Write 6 FAQ items a {city} property owner would realistically search for. Every question AND answer must be about {service} specifically — do NOT ask about unrelated services or other service types. Cover a MIX of topics so the set is not repetitive: (1) what {service} involves or what to expect, (2) how quickly {business} can help in {city}, (3) cost or pricing factors for {service}, (4) how to prepare for or what happens during {service}, (5) prevention or keeping the problem from happening again, and (6) local seasonal timing or regional factors for {service} in this area (the region's weather, storm and flooding patterns, and seasonal freeze and thaw risks). Do not fabricate prices, guarantees, timelines, or credentials. Each item: - question: specific and searchable (8-15 words); at least half naturally include "{city}". - answer: 40-70 words, grounded in real regional context, wrapped in

...

. ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. Do not guarantee specific drying times or that all water and storm damage can be fully reversed. Do not promise insurance claim approval or coverage. Do not claim specific licenses, certifications, or accreditations. Do not make mold health-hazard claims or offer mold remediation, which is handled separately. Do not give unsafe do-it-yourself advice for standing water, sewage, or electrical hazards. Tone: reassuring and professional, with clear urgency for active water and storm emergencies. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing. LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"items": [{"question": "...", "answer": "..."}, ... 6 items total]}

seasonal_calendar.label: Seasonal Calendar (Standalone)
seasonal_calendar.description: 4-step seasonal breakdown of pressure/demand by season for the region. Opt-in for seasonal niches (pest, wildlife, remediation).
